Dear Ms. Leece:
This is in response to your request for advice regarding your
responsibj.l ities as a candidate for elective office under the
Political 
Reform Act (the rta.1tr1.1 Because your request seeks
general guidance regarding the perrnissj-b.Le uses of campaign funds,
we are treati-ng your request as one for infornal assistance
pursuant to Requlation iaazslcl (copy enclosedl .2
OUESTTON
You ran unsuccessfully for school board in November 1989. In
anticipation of a future candidacy, can you use the funds
remaining in the campaigm bank account for the following
expenditures :
1. Subscriptions to periodicals regarding education policy?
2. 
Membership in organizations to benefit your future
cand j.dacy?
3. 
Your future candidacies for office other than for the
school board?

FACTS
You ran unsuccessfully for Newport-Mesa Unified school
District Board of Trustees election j.n November, 1989. Your
campaign bank account now has a balance of approximately $1,000.
These funds were raised after January 1, 1989.
ANALYSI S
Section 85807 Provides:
Upon Leaving any elected office, or at the end of
the postelection reporting period following the
defelt of a candidate for elective office,
whichever occurs 1ast, campaign funds raised after
January L, :-989, under the control of the former
candidite or elected of f i.cer shalI be considered
surplus campaj-gn funds and sha11 be disclosed
pursuant to the reguirernents of ChaPter 4
lcommencing $rith Section 84100) and sha1l be [used]
onfy for the following PurPoses:
(a) The palment of outstandingr campaigrn debts
or elected officer's 
exPenses.
(b) The pro rata repa)rment of contributions'
(c) Donations to any bona fide charitable'
educationaL, civic, religious, or similar tax-
exempt, nonprofit organization, where no
substantial part of the proceeds will have a
naterial financial effect on the forner candidate
or elected officer, 
any nember of his or her
inmediate famify, or his or her campaign treasurer.
(d) Contributions to a political party or
committee so long as the funds are not used to make
contributions in support of or opposition to a
candidate for elective office.
(e) contributions to support or oppose any
candidate for federal office, any candidate for
elective office in a state other than California,
or any bal1ot measure.


Our File No. I -9 0-061
Page 3
During your telephone conversation of February 12, 1990 with
Ms. Lil1y Spitz of our office, you stated that all the funds now
rernaining in your carnpaign bank account were raised after January
1, 1989.- con-equently, section 85807 would dictate that you may
not use the funds remaining in your campaign account to further
your future candidacy, unless the use fa1Is within the specific
provisions of Section 85807.
The expenditures for Periodical subscriptions and
organizational nrenberships outlined-in your guestj-on would be in
aniicipation of running for the office of school board, once
again.- Such expenditures are not specificallY pemitted by
s6cti.on 85807 and are, therefore, prohibited-5 Assuming that you
have no plans to run for federal office, you should follow the
provisio-ns of Section 85807 to disburse the surplus funds
iemai.ning in your campaign account.
If you have any questions regarding this analysis, please
contact ne at (916) 322-5901-.

regulations interpreting these laws. Therefore, it is possible
th;t the Cornmission may adopt regulations in the future which may
be a variance with the advice given in this letter.
